menuWelcome1.en:
It looks like this is your first time playing Beatblock. 
Would you like an overview of the main menu?
menuWelcome1.zh-cn:
看起来这是你第一次玩 Beatblock。
需要为你介绍一下主菜单吗？

menuWelcome2.en:
Great! First up is the "Play" menu.
Here, you can view levels grouped by category. For now you only have one unlocked, but by getting B ranks or above on levels you can unlock more!
menuWelcome2.zh-cn:
好的！首先是“游玩”菜单。
在这里，你可以看到按类别分组的关卡。目前你只解锁了一个，但通过在关卡中获得 B 或更高的评级，你就可以解锁更多内容！

menuWelcome3.en:
Next is "Freeplay".
In this menu, all of your unlocked songs are displayed in a convenient list!
menuWelcome3.zh-cn:
接下来是“自由模式”。
在这个菜单中，你所有已解锁的歌曲都会在一个方便的列表中显示出来！

menuWelcome4.en:
After that is "Custom Levels".
Once you've gotten familiar with Beatblock, come over here to create your own levels, as well as play levels created by the community.
menuWelcome4.zh-cn:
再之后是“自定义关卡”。
当你熟悉了 Beatblock 之后，可以来这里创建你自己的关卡，也可以玩社区玩家创建的关卡。

menuWelcome5.en:
The rest of the options should speak for themselves.
Make sure to head over to "Settings" to configure the game to your liking, or jump right into the game with "Play"!
menuWelcome5.zh-cn:
其余的选项应该都一目了然了。
记得去“设置”里根据你的喜好配置游戏，或者直接点击“游玩”来立刻体验吧！

menuWelcomeNo.en: Sounds good! Head into "Play" to get started unlocking new levels by getting B ranks or above.
menuWelcomeNo.zh-cn: 没问题！前往“游玩”，通过获得 B 或更高的评级来开始解锁新关卡吧。

editorWarning.en:
Hello, thanks for checking out the level editor!
While its usable to create levels, it is currently not very user friendly, and is prone to glitches. 
Additionally, the editor does not support controllers. If you are using a controller, please press [input=back] to quit.
That being said, have fun, and make sure to save often!

(Press enter to go to editor, or escape to quit)
editorWarning.zh-cn:
您好，感谢您试用关卡编辑器！
虽然编辑器可以用来创建关卡，但目前它的用户体验不佳，且容易出现故障。
此外，编辑器不支持手柄操作。如果您正在使用手柄，请按[input=back]键退出。
尽管如此，还是祝您玩得开心，并记得经常保存！

（按 Enter 键进入编辑器，或按 Escape 键退出）

editorKeybinds.en:
Scroll Wheel - Change current time in song
Left, Right - Change selected event rotation (according to current snap)
Up, Down - Change selected event time (according to current snap)
LMB on an event - Select event
RMB on an event - Delete event
LMB or RMB on empty space - Deselect current event
LMB and drag - Move event (works on hold ends too)
Shift + LMB - Multi select events
  Click once to select start point, again to select end point
Alt + LMB + drag - Multi select events (alternate)
  Selects events only in the highlighted area
Alt + Left/Right - Jump to next/previous section
Alt + F4 - Quit Sibelius
Ctrl + LMB - Duplicate clicked event
Ctrl + Scroll Wheel - Zoom in/out
Hold Alt - Show navigation wheel
Shift + [ - Reduce ratemod by 0.1x (min. 0.25x)
Shift + ] - Increase ratemod by 0.1x (max. 2x)

Esc - Exit editor
S - Saves changes
P - Plays level (from current beat)
F - Jump to position of selected event(s)
Shift + P - Plays level (from bookmark before current beat)
Ctrl + P - Plays level (from start)
Ctrl + C - Copies selected events (only if multiselected)
Ctrl + V - Pastes copied events
Ctrl + R - Resets editor window positions
Ctrl + G - Opens "Go to beat" window if closed
Ctrl + I - Opens "Level properties" window if closed
Ctrl + D - Change the variant you're editing

All of the following keybinds only work during playtesting:
P - Return to editor (at current beat)
Esc - Return to editor (at playtest start beat)
Period - Place a marker at the current beat
[ - Reduce ratemod by 0.1x (min. 0.25x)
] - Increase ratemod by 0.1x (max. 2x)

fish_book_description.en:
"Dearest reader,

This book is a compendium of my experiences as a Certified Master Angler.
Over the years, I have caught and catalogued hundreds of fish.

The time has now come for me to pass on my vast knowledge to future
generations. Following the publication of this tome, I will retire from
fishing and begin a new life in the healthcare industry.

When not spending my time at the pier, I was secretly getting a medical
degree, as an elaborate joke. Turns out I'm really good at it, though.

With all that said, I hope this book is useful in your journey. And good luck."

- Rodney Fields, foreword from "Rodney's Fishin' Rounds"
fish_book_description.zh-cn:
“亲爱的读者，

这本书是我作为一名持证钓鱼大师的经验汇编。
多年来，我捕获并记录了数百种鱼类。

现在，是时候将我渊博的知识传给后代了。
在这本巨著出版后，我将从渔界隐退，并在医疗行业开始新的生活。

在码头消磨时光之余，我偷偷地拿到了一个医学学位，
这本是一个精心设计的玩笑。但结果发现，我在这方面还真挺有天赋的。

说了这么多，我希望这本书能在你的旅途中有所帮助。祝你好运。”

- 罗德尼·菲尔兹，《罗德尼的钓鱼巡礼》前言

Its small stature and flexible diet, both of which are uncharacteristic of the Selachii division, boost its pet stats to great heights. Both of these oddities do check out when one considers the years it spent surviving art school.

fish_pianosesolphin_description.en: The Pianose Solphin can be found composing music with its pod and, in its spare time, enjoys arguing over whether "thirty-second note" or "demisemiquaver" is the correct name for the sheet music notation.
fish_pianosesolphin_description.zh-cn: 钢琴独奏海豚常与它的族群一起作曲，闲暇时喜欢争论乐谱上的记号究竟该叫“三十二分音符”还是“半三十二分音符”才是正确的名称。
fish_pianosesolphin_description.owo: The Pianose Solphin can be found composing music with its pod and, in its spare time, enjoys arguing over whether "thirty-second note" or "demisemiquaver" is the correct name for the sheet music notation.

fish_twofish_description.en: Ohhhhh... there's TWO of them...?! Their synchronized and swingin' method of swimming loosely resembles that yin and yang stuff. While I would love to expound upon that, I am a deeply white man.
fish_twofish_description.zh-cn: 哦哦哦……居然有两条……？！它们同步且摇摆的游泳方式隐约有点像那什么阴阳的东西。虽然我很想就此展开讲讲，但我是个彻头彻尾的白人。
fish_twofish_description.owo: Ohhhhh... there's TWO of them...?! Their synchronized and swingin' method of swimming loosely resembles that yin and yang stuff. While I would love to expound upon that, I am a deeply white man.

fish_seaplane_description.en: A good Seaplane is really freakin' hard to catch due to its "big trick", wherein it dives nose-first into the sea floor while emitting smoke - presumably to camoflauge as a natural underwater volcano. It stands to reason that this saltwater fish should stay in saltwater. It instead jumps between bodies of water, or whichever location has the best sunsets.
fish_seaplane_description.zh-cn: 一条优秀的水上飞机鱼真的非常非常难抓，因为它的“大招”是头朝下扎入海底并喷出烟雾——大概是伪装成天然海底火山。按理说这种咸水鱼应该待在咸水里，但它却喜欢在各种水域之间跳跃，或者去任何日落最美的地方。
fish_seaplane_description.owo: A good Seaplane is really freakin' hard to catch due to its "big trick", wherein it dives nose-first into the sea floor while emitting smoke - presumably to camoflauge as a natural underwater volcano. It stands to reason that this saltwater fish should stay in saltwater. It instead jumps between bodies of water, or whichever location has the best sunsets.
